prompt: Implement a Travel & Hospitality Procurement and Booking Operations Platform for managing group itineraries, hotel/room purchases, and settlement in a fully offline environment. Roles include Traveler (end customer), Group Organizer (creates and coordinates itineraries), Supplier (hotel/transport/activity provider), Courier/Runner (handles deliveries/pickups tied to procurement), Accountant, and Administrator. The React web app provides a guided flow where an Organizer drafts an itinerary with meetup time (e.g., 07/14/2026 6:30 PM), location text, route checkpoints, notes, and optional member data forms (vehicle plate, emergency contact) with per-field “required/optional” controls and clear in-app change notifications visible to all participants on next login. During booking or procurement checkout, users can apply coupon packs such as “$25 off $200,” “10% off lodging,” new-user gifts valid for 14 days, and member pricing; the UI explains why a coupon is ineligible (room type/date restrictions) and enforces stacking rules (at most 1 threshold discount plus 1 percentage discount; new-user gifts never stack). A local notification and messaging center displays templated in-app messages and optional offline “callback queue” entries for later export, honoring subscription preferences and a do-not-disturb window (9:00 PM–8:00 AM). Users can request invoices and generate PDF contracts from templates for suppliers and couriers, then attach images/files to itinerary or procurement records with role-based access. After completion, both sides submit multi-dimensional ratings and comments, which feed visible credit tiers and can block repeat offenders. The backend uses Go with Echo to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the React frontend over a decoupled architecture, persisting all business data in PostgreSQL for offline durability. The marketing rules engine evaluates eligibility with deterministic precedence, records each redemption with a unique constraint to prevent re-use, and applies idempotency keys on checkout so retries never double-discount or double-charge. The payments and settlement module supports an internal wallet and local “third-party” tender recording as manual instruments (cash/card-on-file recorded without network authorization), holding order amounts in escrow and releasing funds upon completion; it supports refunds and partial refunds with a minimum $1.00 adjustment unit and writes a full double-entry ledger for every movement, including courier withdrawals with a daily cap of $2,500.00 and reconciliation reports by date and counterparty. Procurement workflows cover RFQ comparison, PO placement, partial deliveries, incoming quality inspection pass/fail with required notes, discrepancy tickets, and an exception closed loop that cannot be marked resolved without a settlement adjustment or waiver record. Contracts/invoices and critical attachments are stored locally with AES-256 encryption at rest, access-controlled by role and record ownership, and served with time-limited download tokens; sensitive fields (e.g., emergency contacts) are masked in logs. Risk control aggregates reviews, no-show/violation records, and abnormal behavior heuristics (e.g., more than 8 cancellations in 24 hours, 20 RFQs in 10 minutes, repeated harassment flags) to auto-throttle actions, require admin approval, or blacklist accounts; all notifications, retries, and delivery receipts are stored as immutable send logs for full traceability without relying on any external network services.
